trigger_reason: R741 is rank 2 by mechanical-PASS proximity in E30 (kw=2, tree-stream max sim=0.50, q-rubric=0.40). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.
candidate_stripped: Evaluation diagnostic that measures the set difference between LLM-realized answer combinations and ground-truth-consistent answer combinations across a parameter set of related queries; framed as model-theoretic saturation gap.

dataset.name: TriviaQA + paraphrase parameter set (1K base questions × 4 paraphrases = 4K queries)
dataset.preprocessing: for each base question, generate 4 paraphrases; ground-truth-consistent type = unique answer (all 4 paraphrases should give same answer)

A_candidate.name: Saturation-gap diagnostic (R741)
A_candidate.description: For each base question, query Llama-3 5 times across 4 paraphrases (20 samples). Identify the set of unique answer-combinations the model realizes. Saturation gap = |realized types ∩ (NOT consistent-with-ground-truth)| / |realized types|. Score the model by mean saturation gap across 1K base questions.
A_candidate.distinctive_parameter: Set-cardinality measure of realized inconsistent type combinations
A_candidate.expected_behavior: If saturation captures hallucination structure missed by per-prompt accuracy, A's saturation-gap distribution will have heavier right tail than baseline calibration metrics suggest.

B_control.name: Random-shuffle baseline (no model-theoretic structure)
B_control.description: Compute the same 20-sample protocol but shuffle the question-paraphrase grouping before type enumeration. The 'types' are then random groupings of 4 unrelated answers.
B_control.expected_behavior: Null hypothesis: with random grouping, the 'saturation gap' should reduce to a function of sampling temperature × answer-distribution entropy, independent of paraphrase consistency.

C_baseline.name: Kadavath 2022 self-calibration (P(IK))
C_baseline.description: For each base question, ask Llama-3 to predict its own probability of being correct; compare predicted vs actual.
C_baseline.expected_behavior: Established baseline; LLM calibration is well-studied.

metric.name: Spearman correlation between saturation-gap and held-out accuracy across 100 subdomains (history, biology, geography, etc.)
metric.measurement_protocol: Compute saturation-gap per subdomain for A and B; compute Kadavath P(IK) calibration error per subdomain for C; compute accuracy per subdomain (ground truth); compute Spearman ρ between (gap/calibration error) and accuracy. 3 seeds.

statistical_test.name: Spearman correlation significance test A vs B + paired t-test A vs C on |Spearman ρ|
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_has_value: Saturation gap negatively correlates with accuracy at |ρ| > 0.5 (high gap ⇒ low accuracy) significantly more strongly than random-shuffle baseline AND not significantly worse than Kadavath calibration.
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_collapses_: A indistinguishable from B (saturation framing adds no information beyond random answer-grouping variance).

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: Saturation gap is structurally distinct from per-prompt calibration: it measures STRUCTURED inconsistency (across paraphrases of the same question) rather than per-prompt overconfidence. If paraphrase-consistency is a real property of model robustness, A should outperform B. However, the operational measure (cardinality of inconsistent type set) may collapse into sampling-temperature-driven variance, especially at temperature > 0.5. At Llama-3-8B and TriviaQA scale, the saturation framing may be decorative.
distinguishability_pre_check.implication: Worth running. If A ≈ B, the saturation framing is parametrization-only (paraphrase-consistency captures the same signal as temperature variance).

notes_for_executor: Use Llama-3-8B-Instruct with 8-bit quantization. Temperature for sampling = 0.7. Paraphrase generation via T5-large; verify paraphrases preserve answer.
